message:
fix(fabric): reserve cell capacity during two-phase publish to prevent overcommit

Previously, prepare_publish only checked cell buffer length against
capacity but did not reserve the slot. Between prepare and apply (or
abort/drop), concurrent publishes could fill the buffer beyond the
configured capacity because they saw uncommitted slots as free.

Changes:
- Add reserved_slots counter to FabricCellRuntime, tracking prepared
  but not yet applied/released publish reservations.
- occupied_len() returns buffer.len() + reserved_slots for accurate
  capacity accounting.
- Rename from_buffer_len to from_occupied_len for clarity.
- Extract refresh_state() method on FabricCellRuntime to centralize
  state transition logic (Empty/Buffered/Backpressured).
- prepare_publish now increments reserved_slots on each routed cell
  after the capacity check, with state transitions traced.
- apply_prepared_publish decrements reserved_slots atomically with
  the buffer push, maintaining the occupied_len invariant.
- New release_prepared_publish method decrements reserved_slots when
  a prepared publish is abandoned (abort or drop), with state
  transitions traced.
- PublishPermit::abort and Drop now call release_prepared_publish to
  free reserved capacity instead of just dropping the PreparedFabricPublish.
- certified_publish_with_obligation releases the reservation on
  obligation allocation or commit failure before returning the error.
- Added tests: reserve_publish_holds_cell_capacity_until_abort,
  reserve_publish_drop_releases_reserved_capacity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
